reasons_to_visit.details: Kraków's Easter market fills the Main Market Square (Rynek Główny) beside the Cloth Hall with painted eggs (pisanki), palm bundles, smoked oscypek cheese, and Polish folk crafts, creating a festive springtime atmosphere in Europe's largest medieval square.

Mild temperatures and lengthening days make April ideal for exploring the UNESCO-listed Old Town, climbing to Wawel Royal Castle, and strolling the Planty park ring as its trees and flowerbeds burst into blossom.

On the Tuesday after Easter, the ancient Rękawka folk festival takes place around Krakus Mound (Kopiec Krakusa) in Podgórze, with Slavic reenactments, early-medieval crafts, and bonfires reviving one of Kraków's oldest spring traditions.

During Holy Week, the acclaimed Misteria Paschalia festival brings Baroque and early-music ensembles to Kraków's historic churches and concert halls, pairing Easter's solemnity with world-class sacred music.
April sits before the summer rush, meaning shorter queues at Wawel Cathedral, St. Mary's Basilica, and Schindler's Factory, while the weather is already pleasant for sightseeing.

The former Jewish quarter of Kazimierz comes alive in spring, with Plac Nowy zapiekanki, café terraces, and historic synagogues making for atmospheric April wandering between Plac Nowy and the Vistula boulevards.
Spring brings żurek soup served in bread, white sausage, mazurek cakes, and Easter specialties to Kraków restaurants such as Miód Malina and Pod Aniołami, showcasing the city's seasonal food culture.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Easter draws many domestic and foreign visitors to Kraków, and many shops, restaurants, and attractions close or reduce hours on Easter Sunday and Monday, with higher accommodation prices around the holiday.
Despite generally mild conditions, April weather in Kraków can be changeable, with sudden cold snaps and showers, so visitors should be prepared for varied conditions.
Early April can still feel chilly, especially in the evenings, with lows near freezing, so warm layers are needed for outdoor activities and the riverside Vistula boulevards.

weather.precipitation: April sees frequent light showers in Kraków, with around 10 rainy days across the month. Showers are usually brief and interspersed with sunshine.
weather.general_tips: Pack a lightweight raincoat or umbrella and dress in layers, as mornings and evenings can still be cold while afternoons warm up.

tourism_level.crowds: Tourist numbers are moderate, rising noticeably around Easter as the Rynek Główny Easter market draws crowds. Locals return to outdoor cafés and the Planty.
tourism_level.pricing: Accommodation prices climb around the Easter holiday but remain reasonable compared with the summer peak.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: The city feels lively and welcoming, with Easter market stalls, blossoming trees, and early-spring energy across the Old Town.
tourism_level.public_transport_crowding: Moderate, higher during the Easter weekend

events_holidays.name: Easter Market on Rynek Główny
events_holidays.description: Traditional Polish Easter market on the Main Market Square featuring painted eggs, palm bundles, oscypek cheese, folk crafts, and seasonal food beside the Cloth Hall.
events_holidays.notes: Visit on a weekday morning for the best selection and fewer crowds. Excellent for photography and tasting regional specialties.
events_holidays.accessibility: Family-friendly and largely accessible across the square.

events_holidays.name: Misteria Paschalia
events_holidays.description: Internationally renowned early-music festival held during Holy Week, presenting Baroque and Renaissance sacred works in Kraków's historic churches and concert venues.
events_holidays.notes: Book tickets in advance, as marquee concerts sell out quickly. A highlight of Kraków's spring cultural calendar.
events_holidays.accessibility: Venue-dependent; most concert halls are accessible.

local_foods_in_season: Żurek (sour rye soup with white sausage)

weather.precipitation: Showers remain possible, but the second half of April brings more sunny days and warmer afternoons.
weather.general_tips: Continue dressing in layers and keep a compact umbrella handy, though afternoons increasingly allow lighter clothing.

tourism_level.crowds: Tourist numbers rise gradually as spring advances and the weather improves, but the city remains far less crowded than in summer.
tourism_level.pricing: Prices are moderate, with some fluctuation around the Easter holiday and late-spring weekends.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: The atmosphere is vibrant, with café terraces full, street performers on the Rynek, and the Planty in full blossom.

events_holidays.name: Easter Sunday and Monday (Śmigus-Dyngus)
events_holidays.description: Polish Easter celebrations with church services at Wawel Cathedral and St. Mary's Basilica, family feasts, and Easter Monday's playful Śmigus-Dyngus water-splashing tradition.
events_holidays.notes: Many shops and restaurants close or reduce hours; book Easter dining and accommodation well in advance.
events_holidays.accessibility: Most church services and gatherings are accessible.

events_holidays.name: Rękawka Festival at Krakus Mound
events_holidays.description: Ancient spring folk festival held on the Tuesday after Easter around Krakus Mound in Podgórze, featuring early-Slavic reenactments, historical crafts, archery, and bonfires.
events_holidays.notes: Wear sturdy shoes for the mound's grassy slopes. A unique, atmospheric Kraków tradition with deep historical roots.
events_holidays.accessibility: Outdoor terrain with slopes; limited wheelchair accessibility.

events_holidays.name: Historic Walking Tours
events_holidays.description: Guided walking tours of Kraków's Old Town, Wawel Hill, and Kazimierz, taking in the Rynek Główny, St. Mary's Basilica, and the former Jewish quarter.
events_holidays.notes: Book tours in advance around Easter. Many tours combine the Old Town with Kazimierz and the Vistula boulevards.
events_holidays.accessibility: Walking required; many streets have cobblestones.

activities: Easter market shopping on Rynek Główny
Wawel Royal Castle and Cathedral tours
St. Mary's Basilica and Veit Stoss altar visit
Historic walking tours of the Old Town
Kazimierz and Plac Nowy zapiekanka tasting
Strolling the blossoming Planty park ring
